'''Patrick Mago''' (born 4 December 1994) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in [[Super League]]. 

He previously played for the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], [[North Queensland Cowboys]], and the [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Background==
Mago was born in [[Otara, New Zealand]], and is of [[Samoans|Samoan]] descent and moved to Australia at a young age. He grew up in [[Logan City]], [[Queensland]] and attended [[Marsden State High School]].

He played his junior football for the [[Souths Logan Magpies]] before signing with the [[Canberra Raiders]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/questnews/logan/seven-graduating-marsden-state-high-school-students-have-signed-with-nrl-clubs/news-story/e8036cbb01e1f05e4ddbafd27b5686b1|title=League's new secret talent school|website=Couriermail.com.au|accessdate=17 August 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ourfootyteam.com/marsdenshs/index.php|title=Marsden SHS Rugby League Locker Room|website=Ourfootyteam.com|accessdate=17 August 2017}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2012, Mago moved to [[Canberra]] and played for Canberra [[SG Ball Cup]] side. In 2013, he moved up to their [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] side. At the end of that season he represented the [[Junior Kangaroos]], scoring twice in the side's 38-26 win over the [[Junior Kiwis]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2013/10/13/eels-shine-in-junior-kangaroos-victory.html|title=Eels Shine in Junior Kangaroos Victory|website=Parraeels.com.au|accessdate=18 August 2017}}</ref> 

In 2014, Mago captained the Raiders' NYC side and was chosen to represent the [[Queensland under-20 rugby league team|Queensland under-20]] and [[Junior Kangaroos]] sides.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/queensland-name-under-20s-origin-team/tabid/10874/newsid/78002/default.aspx|title=Queensland name Under-20s Origin team|website=Nrl.com|accessdate=17 August 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.canberratimes.com.au/rugby-league/canberra-raiders/canberra-raiders-teenager-patrick-mago-playing-for-family-20141022-119zcr.html|title=Canberra Raiders teenager Patrick Mago playing for family|first=Jon|last=Tuxworth|date=24 October 2014|website=Canberratimes.com.au|accessdate=17 August 2017}}</ref>
[[File:Patrick Mago.jpg|thumb|right|Mago playing for the Mounties in 2015]]
In 2015, Mago moved into Canberra's [[NRL]] squad, but spent the season playing for their feeder club, [[Mounties RLFC|Mounties]], in the [[New South Wales Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/newslocal/south-west/sport/nsw-cup-bulldogs-hammer-mounties/news-story/c62398b711642242a9423e47a3aaecab|title=Bulldogs get big bite out of Mounties|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|accessdate=17 August 2017}}</ref> After being released at the end of the season, Mago spent the 2016 pre-season with [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] on a train and trial contract and eventually earned a two-year NRL contract with the club. He spent the season playing for the North Queensland feeder club, the [[Mackay Cutters]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.northernstar.com.au/news/cutters-win-in-a-thriller/3055846/|title=Cutters win in a thriller|first=Chris|last=Lees|website=Northernstar.com.au|accessdate=17 August 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2016/11/07/_2017_cowboys_taking.html|title=2017 Cowboys taking shape|website=Cowboys.com.au|accessdate=17 August 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170813055654/http://www.cowboys.com.au/news/2016/11/07/_2017_cowboys_taking.html|archive-date=13 August 2017|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2017===
After starting the season with the [[Northern Pride RLFC|Northern Pride]], Mago made his first grade debut for North Queensland in their Round 23 game against [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].<ref name="Wests Tigers v South Sydney Rabbitohs">{{cite web|url=https://matchcentre.nrl.com/match/2017/111/20171112350/#team-lists|title=Penrith Panthers v North Queensland Cowboys|publisher=NRL|date=12 August 2017|accessdate=13 August 2017}}</ref> In October, he signed with the Cowboys' Queensland rivals, the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/broncos/why-brisbane-broncos-win-from-adam-blairs-money-move/news-story/7f5899facd667d6c894d02df73e85fd2|title=Adam Blair NRL contract, Warriors signing, Brisbane Broncos salary cap, roster - Fox Sports|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=4 October 2017 |accessdate=27 September 2018}}</ref>

===2018===
Mago made 12 appearances for Brisbane in the [[2018 NRL season]] including the club's elimination final loss against St. George at [[Suncorp Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/sep/09/blistering-first-half-dragons-performance-puts-broncos-out-of-nrl-finals-running |title=Blistering first-half Dragons performance puts Broncos out of NRL finals running |date=2018-09-09 |work=The Guardian |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180909115417/https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/sep/09/blistering-first-half-dragons-performance-puts-broncos-out-of-nrl-finals-running |archive-date=2018-09-09 |agency=Australian Associated Press}}</ref>

===2019 & 2020===
After making no appearances in the [[2019 NRL season]] for Brisbane, Mago joined South Sydney and made 12 appearances for the club in the [[2020 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-10-17/nrl-preliminary-final-penrith-panthers-south-sydney-scorecentre/12774550/|title=Penrith Panthers beat South Sydney Rabbitohs 20-16 to set up NRL grand final against Melbourne Storm|newspaper=ABC News |date=17 October 2020 |publisher=Australian Broadcasting Corporation}}</ref>

===2021===
[[File:PATRICK MAGO.jpg|thumb|right|Mago playing for the South Sydney Rabbitohs in 2021]]
On 15 July 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[Super League]] on a three-year deal for the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Manchester Evening News|date=15 Jul 2021|accessdate=15 Jul 2021|title=Wigan Warriors 'sign' two NRL forwards for 2022|url=https://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-mago-kaide-ellis-21059254}}</ref>

===2022 & 2023===
On 28 May 2022, Mago played for Wigan in their Challenge Cup final victory over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]. Mago made 31 appearances for Wigan throughout the 2022 Super League season including the clubs upset semi-final loss against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]. On 14 October 2023, Mago played in Wigan's [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|newspaper=The Observer|date=14 October 2023 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>

===2024===
On 24 February, Mago played in Wigan's [[2024 World Club Challenge]] final victory over [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68337685|title=World Club Challenge: Wigan Warriors 16-12 Penrith Panthers - Cherry and Whites crowned world champions for fifth time|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 8 June, Mago played in Wigan's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] victory over Warrington from the interchange bench.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 6 September, Mago played just 14 minutes in Wigan's victory over [[Hull KR]] and won man of the match having been credited with turning the game's momentum and ultimately seeing success for Wigan in a decisive game for the [[League Leaders Shield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/powerhouse-prop-patrick-mago-a-point-of-difference-with-inspiring-impact-in-win-over-hull-kr-4773084/|title=Powerhouse prop Patrick Mago a ‘point of difference’ with inspiring impact in win over Hull KR|website=www.wigantoday.net}}</ref>
On 12 October, Mago played in Wigan's 9-2 2024 Super League grand final victory over Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===2025===
On 9 October, Mago played in Wigan's 24-6 2025 Super League Grand Final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===2026===
On 30 May, Mago played in Wigan's 2026 Challenge Cup final victory against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2026/jun/01/challenge-cup-crowds-are-dwindling-but-rugby-league-must-save-wembley-relationship|title=Challenge Cup crowds are dwindling but rugby league must save Wembley relationship|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
